28' · Short fiction · Greece / France / Norway · 2025

Pirateland

As winter begins on an overly touristic Greek island in Crete, a struggling local family running a small bed & breakfast receive an unexpected visit from Norwegian tourists seeking the authentic pirate experience. The relationship between Manos, the Greek father, and Tassos, his son, is tested as their demands grow ever more challenging.

Directed byStavros Petropoulos
Written byYorgos Teltzidis
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos
Co-produced byAlaska Films (GR), Yukunkun Productions (FR), Ferdi Film (NOR), Onassis Culture (GR), GFC (GR)
Supported byARTE France, Region of Creta, Indigo View
CinematographyYorgos Koutsaliaris
Edited byStamos Dimitropoulos
  • Clermont-Ferrand International Film Festival — International Competition — World Premiere
  • Brussels ISFF — International Competition
  • Tribeca Festival — North American Premiere
▶ Watch trailer
80' · Documentary · Greece · 2024

Panellinion

Panellinion is an out-of-place-and-time chess coffeehouse in the centre of Athens — a refuge for people who suffocate in everyday life. Giannis, the owner, hates chess yet looks upon the regulars as his children: scientists, artists, retirees, workers who seek outlet in chess, make confessions, recall old stories of legendary eccentric customers.

Written & directed byKostas Antarachas & Spyros Mantzavinos
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos
Co-produced byAlaska Films, ERT & 2|35
SupportGreek Film Center
CinematographyYorgos Koutsaliaris
Edited byDimitris Polyzos
  • Thessaloniki International Documentary Festival — Best Newcomer Award, Best Greek Film Award, Youth Jury Special Prize
  • Iris 2025 — Hellenic Film Academy Awards — Nominated for Best Documentary
▶ Watch trailer
17' · Short fiction · Greece / Cyprus · 2023

End of August

That summer, Amelia will discover her mother's secret love affair. Refusing to believe what she saw, she runs away from home with Epaminondas, her first love. They will live an adventure, testing themselves and everyone they meet, ending up at the end of the night as children again in the arms of their parents.

Written & directed byAngelika Katsa
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Co-produced byAlaska Films, Felony Film, Ready2Cast, Rentphotovideo, GFC & ERT
CinematographyYorgos Koutsaliaris
Edited byStamos Dimitropoulos
▶ Watch trailer
26' · Short documentary · Greece · 2021

Microbiome

The limits of intrusion in the peaceful lives of the local folk on the island of Ikaria are tested as scientists quest to find the secret to long and healthy life. A clash of energies between locals and perplexed scientists — a comedic study in pacing and reframing a world view.

Written & directed byStavros Petropoulos
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Co-produced byAlaska Films & CLEO
CinematographyYiannis Kanakis
Edited byStamos Dimitropoulos
Original music byDimitris Patsaros
  • FeKK — Grand Prix
  • CPH:DOX — International Premiere
  • Sarajevo International Film Festival — International Competition
▶ Watch trailer
35' · Documentary · Greece · 2019

Homes

Ten site-specific shows and performances examine the traumatic experience of forceful displacement, the violent loss of one's home and the sense of belonging. The film follows artists through Athens and Piraeus creating notional shared spaces on the borderline between fiction and reality.

Written & directed byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Co-produced byAlaska Films & Onassis Cultural Centre
CinematographyIason Arvanitakis
Edited byGiorgos Zafeiris
▶ Watch full film
52' · Documentary · Greece · 2018

X Apartments

Fifteen apartments, two routes in two forgotten neighbourhoods of Athens, two spectators. A documentary about a transmedial group performance and the questions it raises — searching for the boundaries between art and reality, private and public space, voyeurism and intimacy.

Written & directed byLeonidas Konstantarakos
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Co-produced byAlaska Films & Onassis Cultural Centre
CinematographyIason Arvanitakis
Edited byGiorgos Zafeiris
▶ Watch trailer
72' · Documentary · Greece · 2018

I am a Dancer

Four persons with different disabilities come in contact with contemporary dance in a series of workshops organised by the Onassis Cultural Centre. Under the two-year guidance of Greek choreographers, they experiment with movement, discover their potential and contribute to three pieces for the International Unlimited Access Dance Festival.

Written & directed byStavros Petropoulos
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Stavros Petropoulos
Co-produced byAlaska Films & Onassis Cultural Centre
CinematographyYiannis Kanakis
Edited byGiorgos Zafeiris
80' · Documentary · Greece · In production · Est. delivery 2025

Magnifier

Life with someone suffering from Alzheimer's disease resembles the slow-motion crumbling of a building. The sometimes momentary loss of one's face is stretched through time — a gradual denudation where it parts with levels of itself until there's only the core: a naked feeling without any memory. Thekla's documentary places this course in filmic time, having begun filming since the first diagnosis of her mother's disease.

Written & directed byThekla Malamou
Produced byLeonidas Konstantarakos & Thekla Malamou
ProductionAlaska Films
SupportGreek Film Center
CinematographyThekla Malamou
Edited byIoanna Pogiantzi
